title: Rear cargo management systems
abstract: Provided is a cargo management system for a rear cargo compartment of a vehicle. In one embodiment, the cargo management system comprises a cargo management tray supported upon a rail system mounted upon a load floor of the vehicle cargo compartment. In another embodiment, the cargo management system comprises a generally planar positionable cargo barrier, that is selectably positionable in either an upright position on a load floor track, or a generally horizontal position on a seatback track. In another embodiment, the cargo management system comprises a plurality of securable cargo storage containers, having an operable door to enable contents contained therein to be securely stored.
claims:
Claims CLAIMED 1. A cargo management system for a rear cargo compartment of a vehicle, the cargo management system comprising, a cargo management tray supported upon a rail system mounted upon a load floor of the vehicle cargo compartment, wherein the cargo management tray is operable to move from a first position where the cargo management tray is fully contained in the area of the vehicle cargo compartment, and a second position where at least a portion of the cargo management tray extends outwardly of the vehicle through a door opening. 2. The cargo management system according to claim 1, wherein the cargo management tray includes a base and an upstanding perimeter wall that defines a storage receptacle, and a system of dividers arranged to subdivide the storage receptacle into a plurality of storage compartments. 3. The cargo management system according to claim 2, wherein the dividers are moveable to user defined positions within the storage receptacle of the cargo management tray. 4. The cargo management system according to claim 1, wherein the rail system includes at least two rail assemblies positioned on opposing sides of the cargo management tray, the 

 rail assemblies each having a stationary track mounted on the load floor of the vehicle, and a moveable runner that is attached to an underside of the base. 5. The cargo management system according to claim 4, wherein the moveable runner includes a plurality of roller assemblies, each having a pair of rollers configured to be mechanically locked but slidable within the stationary track. 6. The cargo management system according to claim 5, further comprising a slide preventer that maintains the cargo management tray from inadvertent displacement during operation of the vehicle. 7. The cargo management system according to claim 6, wherein the slide preventer is configured as at least one detent provided in the track, the detent cooperating with at least one spring-mounted roller assembly , requiring the user to apply an overcoming force to initiate movement of the cargo management tray. 8 The cargo management system according to claim 7, wherein the rail system incorporates a plurality of detents to define at least the first and second positions, and optionally further detents to define intermediate positions therebetween. 9. The cargo management system according to claim 1, wherein the cargo management tray is removable from the rail system. 10. A cargo management system for a rear cargo compartment of a vehicle, the cargo management system comprising, a generally planar positionable cargo barrier, at least one load floor track provided on a load floor of the cargo compartment, at least one seatback track provided on a rear surface of a rearmost passenter seat, wherein the positionable cargo barrier is selectably positionable in either an upright position on the load floor track, or a generally horizontal position on the seatback track. 11. The cargo management system according to claim 10, wherein the cargo barrier includes a slider mechanism at each interface between the cargo barrier and either of the load floor or seatback tracks. 12. The cargo management system according to claim 11, wherein the slider mechanism is provided with a bearing plate that is mechanically locked but slidable within the load floor track or seatback track. 13. The cargo management system according to claim 12, wherein the slider mechanism additionally includes a lock system that is operable to tighten at least the bearing plate upon an inside surface of the load floor track or seatback track. 14. The cargo management system according to claim 13, wherein upon tightening the lock system, either a locking plate or a bearing edge of the cargo barrier additionally engages an outside surface of the load floor track or seatback track. 15. The cargo management system according to claim 14, wherein the bearing edge of the cargo barrier includes a footing to increase the load bearing surface to prevent downward deflection of the cargo barrier under load in the horizontal position. 16. A cargo management system for a rear cargo compartment of a vehicle, the cargo management system comprising, a plurality of securable cargo storage containers, dimensioned to occupy the cargo compartment of the vehicle, each of the plurality of securable cargo storage containers having an operable door to enable contents contained therein to be securely stored. 17. The cargo management system according to claim 16, wherein each of the cargo storage containers includes a base, a roof component, opposing first and second side walls and a rear wall, and wherein the operable door is situated opposite the rear wall and is accessible through the rear door or hatch of the vehicle. 18. The cargo management system according to claim 17, wherein the operable door is provided in the form of a tamboured hatch having a plurality of slats configured to span each cargo storage container from the first container side wall to the second container side wall. 19. The cargo management system according to claim 18, wherein each of the first and second container side walls include a channel configured to slidingly receive opposing edge portions of the tamboured hatch. 20. The cargo management system according to claim 16, wherein the operable door is provided with a latch to enable the operable door to be secured in the closed position. 21. The cargo management system according to claim 20, wherein the latch includes a lock mechanism, to enable the contents of the cargo storage containers to be secured and locked. 22. The cargo management system according to claim 21, wherein the lock mechanism provided on the latch is a keyed lock mechanism. 23. The cargo management system according to claim 21, wherein the lock mechanism provide on the latch is an electronically actuated lock mechanism. 24. The cargo management system according to claim 23, wherein the electronically actuated lock mechanism is a magnetic or solenoid lock mechanism. 25. The cargo management system according to claim 23, wherein the electronically actuated lock mechanism can be remotely operated.
